title: Scyphanthus stenocarpus
intro: Scyphanthus stenocarpus, also known as the Stenocarpus tree, is a plant species within the family Staphylocarpaceae, native to the rainforests of Australia and New Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Scyphanthus stenocarpus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. In herbal medicine, Scyphanthus stenocarpus is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or decoction to treat respiratory issues and fever, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
